articles.headline: 'Extreme Fear' Hits Crypto Markets After Bitcoin Gives Up $75K Spike
articles.body_snippet: Crypto market sentiment is fading as Bitcoin dipped below $69,000. But predictors on Myriad don't expect a return to all-time low fears.

articles.headline: Crypto.com Slashes Workforce by 12% in 'Enterprise-Wide AI' Pivot
articles.body_snippet: Cryptocurrency exchange Crypto.com is laying off around 180 employees as it shifts focus to AI-driven operations.

articles.headline: Morning Minute: Markets Tumble as Iran War Escalates
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin, gold and stocks all tumbled after several strikes on energy infrastructure. Hyperliquid just brought the S&P 500 onchain. And Kraken has put its IPO on ice.

articles.headline: Bitcoin Dips Under $70K as Stocks Tumble on Hawkish Fed Hold—What’s Next?
articles.body_snippet: Analysts remain cautiously optimistic after the Fed’s hawkish stance, expecting a low volatility regime ahead of the quarterly options expiry.

articles.headline: Can Bitcoin Really Do DeFi? A New Protocol Aims to Find Out
articles.body_snippet: OP_NET is a new protocol that aims to bring smart contracts and decentralized finance directly to Bitcoin transactions.

articles.headline: LA Rideshare Driver Charged With Using $2M in COVID Relief Funds to Buy Crypto
articles.body_snippet: Authorities seized almost 40 BTC as part of the investigation, in which a rideshare driver is accused of wire fraud and money laundering.

articles.headline: Quantum-Ready Bitcoin Prototype Debuts, but Adoption Hurdles Loom
articles.body_snippet: Adopting the model would require miners and users to migrate to a separate “Bitcoin Quantum” blockchain rather than upgrade the existing network.

articles.headline: Nasdaq Wins SEC Approval to Trade Tokenized Securities in Pilot Program
articles.body_snippet: The approval lets Nasdaq test tokenized versions of some stocks and ETFs without moving beyond existing market rails.

articles.headline: OpenClaw Developers Lured in GitHub Phishing Campaign Targeting Crypto Wallets
articles.body_snippet: The phishing campaign lures OpenClaw developers with fake $5,000 token airdrops, then drains wallets through a cloned site with a hidden connection prompt.

articles.headline: Why Bitcoin Is Falling Despite $1.1 Billion in ETF Inflows
articles.body_snippet: Persistent inflation signals and surging oil prices are weighing on risk appetite, even as institutional money has continued to flow.

articles.headline: Coalition Urges OpenAI to Scrap AI Ballot Measure Over Child Safety Concerns
articles.body_snippet: Advocacy groups say the OpenAI-backed measure would limit legal accountability and lock in narrow protections for children.

articles.headline: Algorand Foundation Cuts 25% of Staff as Crypto Industry Layoffs Grow
articles.body_snippet: The organization behind layer-1 blockchain Algorand laid off 25% of its staff due to macroeconomic uncertainty and lower crypto prices.

articles.headline: Trump White House Registers Aliens.gov—Is the UFO File Drop Imminent?
articles.body_snippet: The White House registered aliens.gov, a month after Trump ordered the release of all government UAP and UFO files following a viral Obama moment.

articles.headline: From Coinbase to Ripple: The Biggest Crypto Cases Dumped by Trump's SEC
articles.body_snippet: Under President Trump, the SEC has backed down from nearly all its fights with crypto firms. These are the biggest cases ended so far.

articles.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum Waver as Fed Holds Interest Rates Steady
articles.body_snippet: Inflation has exceeded the central bank’s 2% target for nearly five years, but the Fed held rates firm again as Bitcoin and Ethereum wobbled.

articles.headline: Myriad Traders Slash Spring Rally Chances as Bitcoin, Ethereum Slide
articles.body_snippet: A hotter-than-expected inflation reading pushed crypto prices lower Wednesday—slashing the chances of a broad spring breakout.

articles.headline: You Can Now Trade Official S&P 500 Perpetual Futures via Hyperliquid
articles.body_snippet: The S&P 500 index was licensed to Trade[XYZ], a provider of markets for real-world assets on Hyperliquid for perpetual futures.

articles.headline: Crypto Market Structure Bill to Face Key Vote in April and Must Pass by May, Senators Say
articles.body_snippet: Sen. Bernie Moreno said if the Clarity Act isn’t passed by May, “digital asset legislation will not pass for the foreseeable future.”

articles.headline: Pudgy Penguins Launched A New Game. Crypto Scammers Made A Fake Version
articles.body_snippet: Malwarebytes has warned that a phishing campaign is mimicking the Pudgy World game to steal user credentials

articles.headline: Stripe-Backed Tempo Network Launches With Focus on AI Agent Payments
articles.body_snippet: Tempo, the layer-1 blockchain backed by payments giant Stripe, launched its mainnet with a clear focus on AI agent payments.

articles.headline: Citi Downgrades Crypto Exchange Gemini After Cutting Bitcoin, Ethereum Price Targets
articles.body_snippet: Gemini stock (GEMI) fell 16% on Wednesday following the downgrade and a broader market dip, after Citi cut its Bitcoin and Ethereum targets.

articles.headline: XRP signals 20% price rally amid record Korean exchange withdrawals
articles.body_snippet: Korean traders are pulling XRP off exchanges at a rapid pace, while whale flows signal accumulation seen ahead of past rallies.

articles.headline: FTX Recovery Trust to distribute $2.2B to creditors in March
articles.body_snippet: The fourth round of reimbursements to creditors and former clients of the failed crypto exchange since February 2025 brings the total paid to about $10 billion.

articles.headline: Bitcoin prediction markets see 70% chance BTC price crashes to $55K in 2026
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin bull market optimism has suffered since the October crash, as chances of an extended BTC price drop below $55,000 increase.

articles.headline: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.body_snippet: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.headline: Coinbase Commerce page requests seed phrases, raising security concerns
articles.body_snippet: A Coinbase subdomain linked to its Commerce tool reportedly directed users to a withdrawal page asking to enter their seed phrases, raising concerns among security observers.

articles.headline: OP_NET launches Bitcoin DeFi push without bridges or wrapped BTC
articles.body_snippet: OP_NET has launched a “SlowFi” DeFi stack that runs smart contracts directly in standard Bitcoin transactions with BTC as the only gas asset, avoiding bridges and wrapped BTC.

articles.headline: Hacked crypto tokens drop 61% on average and rarely recover, Immune report says
articles.body_snippet: Beyond immediate losses, attacks often lead to prolonged downtime, liquidity shocks and confidence erosion, as interconnected DeFi systems amplify the impact across markets.

articles.headline: Apex Group’s Tokeny taps Polygon to debut chain for compliant tokenized RWAs
articles.body_snippet: Apex Group’s Tokeny and Polygon Labs are launching T-REX Ledger, a Polygon-based blockchain that aims to centralize compliance for ERC-3643 security tokens.

articles.headline: Crypto.com cuts 12% of staff as it accelerates AI push
articles.body_snippet: Crypto.com CEO Kris Marszalek announced a 12% workforce reduction due to AI integrations, warning that companies “that do not make this pivot immediately will fail.”

articles.headline: Bitcoin tests old 2021 top as gold falls to six-week lows under $4.7K
articles.body_snippet: Bitcoin price correction reversed at $69,500, preserving a new higher BTC trading range as gold led a post-Fed macro asset sell-off.

articles.headline: The era of listings and on-ramps is ending, as intent protocols make access native
articles.body_snippet: Listings and on-ramps monetized access scarcity. Intent-based protocols make it native to networks, ending gatekeeper detours forever.

articles.headline: Bitcoin ETF inflow streak snaps with $164M outflows amid BTC dip
articles.body_snippet: US Bitcoin and altcoin ETFs saw notable outflows on Wednesday as BTC dipped below $71,000 and investor sentiment returned to extreme fear.

articles.headline: Ethereum long squeeze risk rises as ETH price dips to $2,100
articles.body_snippet: Ether risks over $2.5 billion in long liquidations below $2K, with volatility increasing the chance of a retest of $1,800 support.

articles.headline: Bitcoin whales shift $100M+ as oil spike rattles markets
articles.body_snippet: Ancient Bitcoin holders moved millions to exchanges as Middle East tensions drove oil higher, fueling a broader risk-off shift across crypto and traditional markets.

articles.headline: South Korea opposition party pushes to scrap planned 22% crypto tax
articles.body_snippet: The ruling Democratic Party responded, saying it has not formed a consensus on abolishing the tax but will review the new proposal.

articles.headline: Why Ethereum developers want ‘one-click staking’ for institutions
articles.body_snippet: Ethereum developers are pushing one-click staking to simplify validator operations, attract institutions and strengthen decentralization across the network.

articles.headline: ECB opens digital euro work on ATMs and payment terminals
articles.body_snippet: The initiative targets how a digital euro would operate across existing payment rails, with emphasis on offline transactions and interoperability.

articles.headline: OpenClaw devs targeted by phishing scam promising free ‘CLAW’ tokens
articles.body_snippet: A phishing campaign used fake GitHub posts and a bogus “CLAW” token to lure OpenClaw developers into connecting crypto wallets.

articles.headline: The abundance that AI may promise is not free
articles.body_snippet: AI abundance promises free everything through massive centralized infrastructure. Whoever controls the energy and AI factories dictates distribution terms and user autonomy.

articles.headline: Canada revokes 47 crypto money licenses, vows to continue
articles.body_snippet: Near the end of last year, FINTRAC fined crypto platform Cryptomus $126 million and crypto exchange KuCoin $14 million for a range of alleged violations.

articles.headline: Visa and Stripe-backed Tempo unveil AI agent payment tools on same day
articles.body_snippet: AI agent users received new tools from Visa and the Stripe-backed Tempo to provide a new way for agentic payments to take place online.

articles.headline: Retail tripled gold buying in last 6 months as Wall Street sells
articles.body_snippet: “Retail-driven exuberance” in gold and silver ETFs and leveraged positions led to the end of the precious metals rally, reported the BIS.

articles.headline: Dorsey’s Block laid off 4K employees last month, some are already back
articles.body_snippet: Block CEO Jack Dorsey said at the time of the layoffs that the company would have erred in some of the decisions it made and that it would correct mistakes.

articles.headline: Crypto traders eye ‘bullish relief rally’ after Fed holds rates steady
articles.body_snippet: Expectation of a relief rally persisted despite a widely used crypto sentiment indicator falling back into “Extreme Fear” on Wednesday.

articles.headline: XRP treasury Evernorth files with SEC to list shares on Nasdaq
articles.body_snippet: Evernorth is moving closer to a public listing after filing a Form S-4 with the SEC, the final major regulatory hurdle of its SPAC merger plan.

articles.headline: Polymarket buys DeFi startup Brahma in latest acquisition wave
articles.body_snippet: “Building reliable infrastructure across blockchain networks and traditional financial rails is hard,” said Polymarket CEO Shayne Coplan.

articles.headline: California court dismisses Coinbase user’s challenge to IRS summons
articles.body_snippet: Another crypto user sued the IRS in 2025 for allegedly violating his Fourth Amendment rights after the agency used a John Doe summons to collect his data from a crypto exchange.

articles.headline: SEC gives go-ahead to Nasdaq for tokenized trading trial
articles.body_snippet: Nasdaq has been given the regulatory green light to offer some tokenized stocks, which will trade alongside traditional securities on its exchange.

articles.headline: Algorand Foundation cuts 25% of staff, citing macro uncertainty
articles.body_snippet: The Algorand Foundation said it has a “more sustainable alignment” of resources with the protocol’s long-term business priorities.

articles.headline: Fold Q4 revenue up, CEO sees Bitcoin rewards overtaking air miles
articles.body_snippet: Fold CEO Will Reeves said it is focused on scaling its 2026 product line after paying off two convertible debts, removing overhang and enabling it to focus on growth.
